top_signals.title: Bitcoin plunges below $66,000 as global stocks, AI trades hit fresh records
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Tony Blair has issued a call to arms – but will Labour listen?
top_signals.summary: Tony Blair’s blockbuster intervention is a reminder of a time when the government believed business was part of the solution to Britain’s problems, says Steve Rigby For entrepreneurs, looking back can be anathema. Everything is about the future – the next customer, the next acquisition, the carefully-plotted route towards a big-money exit.  But for those [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Chinese Investors Exit Hong Kong Stocks as AI Woos Money Onshore
top_signals.summary: Chinese investors are exiting Hong Kong‑listed stocks in record numbers, highlighting waning appetite as mainland AI shares broaden their appeal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Is it time to change how we measure inflation?
top_signals.summary: Measures of inflation are hugely influential on policy but struggle to account for the pace of technological innovation, says Paul Ormerod During this decade, the rate of inflation has become a key factor in determining living standards. Many benefits are linked to it. It sets a marker for wage demands. And when it goes up, [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
